name: Jeollabuk-do (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: the fertile rice fields of the Honam plain, the breadbasket of Korea
purity and the bright future of the province
the Yellow Sea coast and the rivers that water the province
the harvest of grains and the warmth of the sun

description: A green field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem combining curved forms and the wordmark 'JEONBUK', with white and blue accents.
history: North Jeolla Province (Jeollabuk-do, also known as Jeonbuk), reorganised as the Jeonbuk State Special Self-Governing Province on 18 January 2024, has used a series of provincial flags. The most recent emblem stylises the abundance of the Honam plain — Korea's principal rice-producing region — together with the rivers and coast that shape the province.

features.symbolism: the fertile rice fields of the Honam plain
abstract motif evoking the Honam rice plain, the rivers and the Yellow Sea coast that frame the province; expresses prosperity and the dynamic future of Jeonbuk State
